root_cause.text: The HTTP chunk size parsed in `http_receive_data` was not validated against negative values, allowing an attacker to supply a negative chunk size that bypassed bounds checks and led to a heap-based buffer overflow.
root_cause.evidence: The patch changes the chunk size validation from `if (c->chunk_size == 0)` to `if (c->chunk_size <= 0) { c->chunk_size = 0; goto fail; }` to properly reject invalid or negative chunk sizes.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The HTTP chunk size parsed in `http_receive_data` was not validated against negative values, allowing an attacker to supply a negative chunk size that bypassed bounds checks and led to a heap-based buffer overflow.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch changes the chunk size validation from `if (c->chunk_size == 0)` to `if (c->chunk_size <= 0) { c->chunk_size = 0; goto fail; }` to properly reject invalid or negative chunk sizes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Guard against invalid negative HTTP chunk sizes during transfer-encoding parsing.
intent_record.what: Changed the chunk size condition from exactly equal to zero to less than or equal to zero, and set the chunk size to zero before failing.
intent_record.how: Prevents the processing of invalid or negative HTTP chunk sizes, treating them as stream termination or parsing failures.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an assertion to prevent chunk size underflow when receiving HTTP data.
intent_record.what: Added an assertion to check that the length of the received data is not greater than the remaining chunk size.
intent_record.how: Forces the program to abort if 'len' exceeds 'c->chunk_size', ensuring that the subsequent subtraction does not result in an underflow.

grouping_result.intent: Add an assertion to prevent chunk size underflow when receiving HTTP data.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add assertions and guards to prevent underflow or invalid negative HTTP chunk sizes during data transfer.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding guards and assertions to prevent invalid negative HTTP chunk sizes and underflows during data transfer.

candidates.rationale: This line parses the HTTP chunk size from the input buffer without verifying if the parsed value is negative, directly leading to the vulnerability.
This check only verifies if the chunk size is zero, missing a check for negative values which allows the negative chunk size to propagate.
This arithmetic operation uses the unvalidated chunk size, which can lead to unexpected behavior or underflow when processing negative chunk sizes.

vuln_statements.explanation: This line parses the HTTP chunk size from the input buffer without verifying if the parsed value is negative, directly leading to the vulnerability.
This check only verifies if the chunk size is zero, missing a check for negative values which allows the negative chunk size to propagate and cause a heap-based buffer overflow.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Update to work with chunked encoding HTTP streams (as served by FFmpeg since
a few months now). Fixes issue 1738.

Origi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 19c8c4ecf4e26762327a472dcf34132ceb196e30
Author: Ronald S. Bultje <rsbultje@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 3 18:41:50 2010 +0000

    Update to work with chunked encoding HTTP streams (as served by FFmpeg since
    a few months now). Fixes issue 1738.
    
    Originally committed as revision 22175 to svn://svn.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg/trunk

diff --git a/ffserver.c b/ffserver.c
index 87f162fbbe..9a53e4950e 100644
--- a/ffserver.c
+++ b/ffserver.c
@@ -124,6 +124,8 @@ typedef struct HTTPContext {
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the parsing of HTTP chunk sizes using `strtol` into `c->chunk_size` but fails to check if the parsed size is negative. This allows negative values to be passed to `FFMIN(c->chunk_size, c->buffer_end - c->buffer_ptr)` and subsequently to `recv()`, which interprets the negative value as a large unsigned size_t, leading to a heap-based buffer overflow.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Changed a bunch of calls to sprintf to snprintf to protect against buffer
overflows.

Fix streaming from non-streaming f
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit d445a7e9cc31b94ab1eceb228a7634c79d37496e
Author: Philip Gladstone <philipjsg@users.sourceforge.net>
Date:   Wed Nov 19 02:23:17 2003 +0000

    Changed a bunch of calls to sprintf to snprintf to protect against buffer
    overflows.
    
    Fix streaming from non-streaming ffm files. It turned out that you
    always got 'index & id do not match' errors.
    
    Add some more error detection on getting FFM feeds
    
    Originally committed as revision 2523 to svn://svn.ffmpeg.org/ffmp
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided function body does not contain any logic for parsing HTTP chunk sizes. It appears to be handling raw feed packets directly, so the vulnerable construct related to chunk size parsing is absent.

Update to work with chunked encoding HTTP streams (as served by FFmpeg since
a few months now). Fixes issue 1738.

Origi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 19c8c4ecf4e26762327a472dcf34132ceb196e30
Author: Ronald S. Bultje <rsbultje@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 3 18:41:50 2010 +0000

    Update to work with chunked encoding HTTP streams (as served by FFmpeg since
    a few months now). Fixes issue 1738.
    
    Originally committed as revision 22175 to svn://svn.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg/trunk

diff --git a/ffserver.c b/ffserver.c
index 87f162fbbe..9a53e4950e 100644
--- a/ffserver.c
+++ b/ffserver.c
@@ -124,6 +124,8 @@ typedef struct HTTPContext {
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces chunked encoding support and parses the chunk size using `strtol(c->buffer, 0, 16)`. However, it only checks if `c->chunk_size == 0` and fails to validate against negative values, which can lead to negative chunk sizes being passed to `FFMIN()` and subsequently to `recv()`, introducing the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.reason: The provided function body does not contain any logic for parsing HTTP chunk sizes (which is the root cause of the vulnerability). It only handles reading raw data into a buffer, checking for FFM packet signatures, and writing to a feed. Since the vulnerable chunk size parsing construct is completely absent, the bug does not exist in this snippet.
